title: Report: Celtics are ‘known to be interested’ in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics are reportedly interested in trading for Giannis Antetokounmpo as his situation in Milwaukee becomes uncertain.
Giannis has a contract through 2028, but his possible trade value may be explored due to the Bucks' struggles.
Financial challenges exist for Celtics, as Giannis would increase luxury tax burden alongside Tatum and Brown's salaries.

title: 5 takeaways as Red Sox pull away from Brewers, 5-0, to earn first series win
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Red Sox secured their first series win of the season, defeating the Brewers 5-0 on Wednesday.
Sonny Gray pitched 6 1/3 scoreless innings, lowering his ERA to 2.76 and earning a standing ovation.
Tyler Samaniego made a memorable MLB debut, striking out the side in the eighth inning for Boston.

title: Wu releases $4.9 billion budget proposal amid ‘difficult moment’ for city’s finances
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston Mayor Michelle Wu unveiled a $4.9 billion budget proposal for fiscal year 2027, increasing spending by 2.1%.
Key financial challenges include $48.4 million deficit, rising health insurance costs, and city's dependency on reserve funds.
Wu aims to negotiate municipal unions' healthcare costs and address public safety overtime as potential long-term solutions.

title: Rob Gronkowski joins Logan Mankins, Adam Vinatieri on Patriots Hall of Fame ballot
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Rob Gronkowski, Logan Mankins, and Adam Vinatieri are finalists for the Patriots Hall of Fame class.
Fans will vote to determine which finalist gets inducted; results announced after April 28.
Gronkowski is a first-time nominee, Mankins a three-time finalist, Vinatieri a two-time finalist.

title: Bruins sign top prospect James Hagens: How can he help Boston’s playoff push?
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins signed 19-year-old prospect James Hagens to a three-year entry-level contract starting in the 2025-26 season.
Hagens, who played six games for the Providence Bruins, could debut at TD Garden with Boston's playoff aspirations in sight.
Hagens's offensive skills may bolster Boston's depth and power play as they prepare for the Stanley Cup Playoffs.

title: Sen. Markey, local clergy call for end to Iran war and Trump’s time in office
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Senator Ed Markey called for ending U.S. involvement in the Iran war, citing Trump's rhetoric as inflammatory.
Markey expressed support for a war powers resolution to limit presidential authority and criticized Trump as unfit to serve.
Faith leaders and activists joined Markey in urging for a peaceful resolution and condemning U.S. military actions in Iran.

title: Readers say their electric bills are ‘outrageously high.’ Here’s how much they pay.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: 200 of 239 Boston.com readers report their electricity bills are becoming unmanageable.
Some Massachusetts residents claim their electric bills have doubled due to increased rates and additional fees.
Municipal utility providers offer significantly lower rates, but access is limited to 50 communities in Massachusetts.

title: A Nordic-inspired sauna just opened in the White Mountains
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: White Mountain Sauna Haus opened in North Conway, N.H., featuring two Finnish saunas, three plunge pools, and a Nordic-inspired cafe.
The sauna, in a renovated 1800s barn, offers views of Cathedral Ledge and White Horse Ledge from a 1,000 sq ft deck.
Visitors enjoy beer, snacks, and a communal environment with no phones allowed; entry costs $36 per visit.

title: What Patriots coach Mike Vrabel said about viral photos with NFL reporter
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots coach Mike Vrabel dismissed speculation about viral photos with NFL insider Dianna Russini, calling them 'completely innocent.'
Vrabel and Russini were photographed together in Arizona, sparking media coverage in the New York Post's gossip column, Page Six.
Russini and The Athletic's executive editor stated the photos lacked context, emphasizing they were part of a larger social group.

title: The perfect spring weekend starts with daffodils
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: State parks in Massachusetts open for camping, offering information on costs, reservations, and pet rules.
JetBlue raises checked bag fees by $4 during off-peak and $9 during peak periods for economy passengers.
Complimentary tickets to the Paul Revere House available at the Revere Hotel Boston Common with the 'Revere Revolution 250 Package'.
